reasoning: The USD/MXN exchange rate has experienced a strong downward trend throughout late 2025, reaching ~17.88 by early January 2026 as the Mexican Peso strengthened due to favorable interest rate differentials and anticipation of further Fed rate cuts. Short-term momentum strongly suggests a continuation of this downward drift, resulting in a probability below 50% for an increase by Jan 11 (44%). However, for longer horizons (Feb 3 and Apr 4), predicting exchange rates increasingly resembles a random walk, pulling probabilities closer to 50%. Furthermore, asymmetric upside risks exist for the USD/MXN pair—such as the January 31 deadline for a US-Mexico water treaty and the looming USMCA review—which could weigh on the MXN and cause the exchange rate to increase. Consequently, the probability of an increase rises to 47% by Feb 3 and 49% by April 4, reflecting increasing uncertainty and a balance between macro downside pressure (rate cuts) and geopolitical upside risks (tariffs).
